methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

private.name: Classiq
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Riverlane is a Series C quantum computing company founded by Steve Brierley that has raised $120M+ to date. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peer density within quantum computing—only 0 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage comparable met screening criteria—peer-median valuation was not applicable. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity across technology maturity and commercialization phases. Consequently, valuation relied on stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $120M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $204M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $173M to $235M, with a median point of $204M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.7x multiplier to Riverlane's specific capital efficiency and market conditions, the representativeness of total raised as a valuation proxy absent disclosed fundraising price data, and whether quantum computing's wider peer set would materially narrow the range. Completion of a future fundraise or disclosure of recent transaction multiples would refine this estimate.
